title: baccharis genistelloides subsp. crispa
intro: Baccharis genistelloides subsp. crispa, also known as a shrub or small tree native to South America, is a plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Baccharis genistelloides subsp. crispa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Baccharis genistelloides subsp. crispa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
